scenes.title: Welcome + Training Intent
scenes.objective: Set expectations for instructor-led certification delivery and classroom flow.
scenes.script: Today is instructor-led. The projector stays clean; your questions are welcome. We will move in short blocks: explain, demo, practice, verify.
steps: Explain how the day is structured
Explain how skills will be verified
ask: What’s one thing that makes equipment training go sideways on a jobsite?
watchFor: Students treating the day as passive lecture instead of skills verification
scenes.notes: Keep it short. Get them into the rhythm: explain → demo → practice → verify.

scenes.title: Safety Frame: Consequences + Culture
scenes.objective: Anchor the training in real-world risk and shared responsibility.
scenes.script: This is about hazard recognition and decision-making. Equipment doesn’t forgive shortcuts. We’re building habits you can carry to any site.
steps: Define the difference between rules vs habits
Set a ‘see something, say something’ expectation
ask: What does ‘complacency’ look like on equipment?
watchFor: Jokes minimizing risk; redirect to real consequences without shaming
scenes.notes: Keep tone firm. Do not lecture forever. Move to the next skill block quickly.

scenes.title: Skill Verification Overview
scenes.objective: Explain how you will evaluate: what counts, what fails, and what remediation looks like.
scenes.script: We are verifying skill, not vibes. You’ll get guided practice, then a check. If something is unsafe, we stop and re-train.
steps: Explain how coaching happens before verification
Explain what happens on an unsafe act
ask: What do you want the instructor to correct immediately if you start doing it?

items: Unsafe act: stop immediately, reset, explain why, re-demo, re-attempt
Verification: instructor observes without coaching unless safety requires intervention

scenes.notes: This is your ‘contract’ for the rest of the day. Make it clear.
